```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parâmetros aleatórios
n           <- sample(c(25, 36, 50, 100), 1)
mu_true     <- sample(c(2, 4, 6, 8), 1)
sigma2_true <- sample(c(1, 2, 4, 9), 1)

sum_x  <- round(n * mu_true + rnorm(1, 0, 1), 2)
sum_x2 <- round(n * (mu_true^2 + sigma2_true) + rnorm(1, 0, 2), 2)

xbar    <- round(sum_x / n, 4)
s2_mle  <- round(sum_x2 / n - xbar^2, 4)

## Resposta correta
correct <- s2_mle

## Alternativas incorretas plausíveis
w1 <- round((sum_x2 - n * xbar^2) / (n - 1), 4)
w2 <- round(sum_x2 / n, 4)
w3 <- round(sum_x2 / (n - 1) - xbar^2, 4)
w4 <- round(sum_x2 / n - xbar^2 + 1 / n, 4)

## Garantir unicidade
if (w1 == correct) w1 <- round(w1 + 0.05, 4)
if (w2 == correct) w2 <- round(sum_x2 / n + 0.1, 4)
if (w2 == w1)      w2 <- round(sum_x2 / n - 0.1, 4)
if (w3 == correct || w3 == w1 || w3 == w2) w3 <- round(w3 + 0.1, 4)
if (w4 == correct || w4 == w1 || w4 == w2 || w4 == w3) w4 <- round(correct + 0.5, 4)

## Embaralhamento SEMPRE
all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========

Seja $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} N(\mu, \sigma^2)$ com ambos os parâmetros desconhecidos. Com $n = `r n`$, $\bar{X} = `r xbar`$ e $\sum_{i=1}^n X_i^2 = `r sum_x2`$. O EMV de $\sigma^2$ é $\hat{\sigma}^2_{MV} = \frac{1}{n}\sum(X_i - \bar{X})^2 = \frac{\sum X_i^2}{n} - \bar{X}^2$. Calcule $\hat{\sigma}^2_{MV}$.

Answerlist
----------

* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========

Usando a identidade $\sum(X_i - \bar{X})^2 = \sum X_i^2 - n\bar{X}^2$:
$$\hat{\sigma}^2_{MV} = \frac{\sum X_i^2}{n} - \bar{X}^2 = \frac{`r sum_x2`}{`r n`} - (`r xbar`)^2 = `r round(sum_x2/n, 4)` - `r round(xbar^2, 4)` = `r correct`.$$
O divisor do EMV é $n$ (não $n-1$): este estimador é viesado, com $E(\hat{\sigma}^2_{MV}) = \frac{n-1}{n}\sigma^2$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap3S2Q8_emv_normal_ambos
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
